evidence.scientific_clarity: Scatter + OLS fit + R²=0.877 annotation + OursModel emphasized point all present and legible; brief fully satisfied.
evidence.typography: pdffonts shows Type 3 / DejaVu Sans fonts — not editable Type 42; script never sets pdf.fonttype=42; DejaVu Sans is the penalized default.
evidence.palette: Blue (#6baed6) for models and orange-red (#e6550d) for OursModel are colorblind-distinguishable; red OLS line is directionally neutral here; no green/red directional misuse.
evidence.layout_density: tight_layout() applied, 6.5×4.5 figure fills panel well, no gaping whitespace, annotation box and legend placed in opposite corners without overlap.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure exactly; legend top-left, annotation box top-right; OursModel labeled with arrow; numerical findings (slope, R², coords) provided.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type 3 fonts (not Type 42 TrueType); text is not selectable or editable in the PDF; pdf.fonttype=42 never set in the script.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial; figure.svg also present as bonus.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states the scaling law context, cites n=80, gives the OLS equation (y = -0.0449x + 0.621), R²=0.877, and OursModel coordinates — specific and complete.

overall_strengths: Scientific content is complete: scatter, OLS line, R² annotation, and emphasized OursModel point match the brief exactly.
Caption is exemplary: includes equation, R² value, sample size, and OursModel coordinates with precise numerical claims.
overall_weaknesses: PDF font type is Type 3 (bitmap-like) instead of Type 42 (TrueType); text is not selectable/editable — pdf.fonttype=42 was never set.
Typography uses the matplotlib default DejaVu Sans font family, which is not a publication-quality typeface choice.
